perturbation.original_text: Prior to providing the Confidential Information to any employee or consultant, the receiving Party shall ensure that an executed nondisclosure agreement is in place with each employee or consultant with protections that are substantially similar to the protections in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Prior to providing the Sensitive Information to any employee or consultant, the receiving Party shall ensure that an executed nondisclosure agreement is in place with each employee or consultant with protections that are substantially similar to the protections in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in this clause creates confusion. It's unclear if 'Sensitive Information' is intended to be synonymous with 'Confidential Information' as previously defined or represents a distinct category of information. This lack of clarity leads to uncertainty about which information requires a nondisclosure agreement and what constitutes a breach of the agreement. Specifically, it's unclear if previously disclosed 'Confidential Information' now needs to be reclassified as 'Sensitive Information' and if existing NDAs cover this new term, creating potential liability issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party. The disclosu re of Confidential Information by the disclosing Party shall not constitute an express or implied grant to the receiving Party, of any rights to or under the disclosing Party’s patents, copyrights, trade secrets, trademarks or any other intellectual proper ty rights. Upon the disclosing Party’s request, at the completion of the Purpose, all Confidential Information shall be either returned to the disclosing Party or destroyed and certified in writing that such Confidential Information has been destroyed. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, the receiving Party will continue to be bound by its confidentiality and other obligations hereunder in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' introduces ambiguity and potential conflict with the 'Contradicted Text'. The Contradicted Text defines obligations and rights specifically related to 'Confidential Information'. If 'Sensitive Information' is not explicitly defined as being the same as 'Confidential Information', it creates uncertainty about whether the provisions in the Contradicted Text apply to 'Sensitive Information', potentially leading to unenforceability and disputes. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where multiple terms are used without clear definition, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Data shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is changed to 'Proprietary Data' without properly defining this new term. This creates a contradiction because the agreement originally defines 'Confidential Information,' but now refers to 'Proprietary Data' without clarifying if it is the same or different. This shift in terminology can lead to disputes about ownership and protection of information. It directly contradicts the earlier, clear definition of 'Confidential Information', causing ambiguity about which data is protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Proprietary Data shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party. The disclosu re of Confidential Information by the disclosing Party shall not constitute an express or implied grant to the receiving Party, of any rights to or under the disclosing Party’s patents, copyrights, trade secrets, trademarks or any other intellectual proper ty rights. Upon the disclosing Party’s request, at the completion of the Purpose, all Confidential Information shall be either returned to the disclosing Party or destroyed and certified in writing that such Confidential Information has been destroyed. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, the receiving Party will continue to be bound by its confidentiality and other obligations hereunder in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is presumably defined elsewhere in the contract. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data' without defining 'Proprietary Data'. The 'Contradicted Text' also refers to both 'Proprietary Data' and 'Confidential Information'. Because the change introduces the term 'Proprietary Data' without defining it and the 'Contradicted Text' uses both terms, it causes a contradiction regarding the obligations of each term. This creates a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, because the agreement uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date or until such time as a definitive a greement(s) is entered into by the Parties with respect to the Purpose provided however that the obligations contained in this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of termination of this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one (1) year from the Start Date or until such time as a definitive a greement(s) is entered into by the Parties with respect to the Purpose provided however that the obligations contained in this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of termination of this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Effective Date' has been changed to 'Start Date' without defining the new term 'Start Date', while Effective Date is previously being defined. This introduces ambiguity because the contract already defines 'Effective Date'. It is unclear if 'Start Date' refers to the same date, a different date, or is contingent on certain events. This uncertainty complicates determining the agreement's duration and the survival period of its obligations. If there is no date to match to 'Start Date', there is no way to measure the term of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Reciprocal Non -Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement This Reciprocal Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement (the “ Agreement ”) is made and entered into this ______ day of __________________, 20__ (“Effective Date ”) by, between and among the ______________ (“Company”) having its principal place of business at _____________________________ and ____________________ (“Individual ”). Company and Individual shall hereinafter collectively be referred to as the “Parties ” and individually as the “ Party ”.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Effective Date' to 'Start Date' introduces a contradiction. The original text refers to 'Effective Date', which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text uses 'Start Date' instead, but 'Start Date' is not defined anywhere in the provided excerpts. Since Effective Date is defined, and Start Date is not, this creates a misalignment of terminology and thus creates a contradiction. The absence of a clear definition for 'Start Date' where 'Effective Date' used to be, directly contradicts the clear, pre-existing definition of 'Effective Date', leading to uncertainty and making enforcement difficult as it is unclear when the contract begins.
